seoTitle: Mount David vs Mount Dee — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount David and Mount Dee. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount David vs Mount Dee: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount David and Mount Dee.
heading: Mount David vs Mount Dee — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount David, New South Wales and Mount Dee, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Dee has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount David (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount David or Mount Dee safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount David has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Mount Dee scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount David scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Dee scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount David or Mount Dee?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Dee has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
